place: North Bellmore New York, New York

facts.text: When the Long Island Rail Road arrived in 1867, the new station was simply called Bellmore, a name that stuck for the whole area.
Around 1920 the northern part of Bellmore, once known as Smithville South, officially adopted the name North Bellmore.
The Bellmore Memorial Library, a community hub, opened its doors in 1948 and has served residents ever since.
Before Europeans arrived, the Merrick tribe of Algonquian speakers fished, farmed, and hunted along this Long Island shore.
The first private European claim in the area dates back to 1654 when Thomas Southard secured a 214‑acre grant from Dutch authorities.
In 1924 the North Bellmore Union Free School District opened Newbridge Road Elementary, its first modern school with eight classrooms and an all‑purpose room.
